sections.text: Thus, since the Torah and its commandments “clothe” all ten faculties of the soul with all its 613 organs from head to foot, it [the soul] is altogether truly bound up in the Bundle of Life with G–d,18 I Samuel 25:29. and the very light of G–d envelops and clothes it from head to foot, as it is written, “G–d is my Rock, I will take refuge in Him,”19 Psalms 18:3. and it is also written, “You will envelop him with favor (ratzon—will) as with a shield,”20 Psalms 5:13. that is to say, with His will and wisdom, blessed be He, which are clothed in His Torah and its commandments.
sections.text_simple: Thus, since the Torah and its commandments “clothe” all ten faculties of the soul with all its 613 organs from head to foot, it [the soul] is altogether truly bound up in the Bundle of Life with G–d,18 I Samuel 25:29. and the very light of G–d envelops and clothes it from head to foot, as it is written, “G–d is my Rock, I will take refuge in Him,”19 Psalms 18:3. and it is also written, “You will envelop him with favor (ratzon—will) as with a shield,”20 Psalms 5:13. that is to say, with His will and wisdom, blessed be He, which are clothed in His Torah and its commandments.

sections.aramaic: וְלָכֵן אָמְרוּ: ״יָפָה שָׁעָה אַחַת בִּתְשׁוּבָה וּמַעֲשִׂים טוֹבִים בָּעוֹלָם הַזֶּה מִכָּל חַיֵּי עוֹלָם הַבָּא״, כִּי עוֹלָם הַבָּא הוּא שֶׁנֶּהֱנִין מִזִּיו הַשְּׁכִינָה, שֶׁהוּא תַּעֲנוּג הַהַשָּׂגָה, וְאִי אֶפְשָׁר לְשׁוּם נִבְרָא, אֲפִילוּ מֵהָעֶלְיוֹנִים, לְהַשִּׂיג, כִּי אִם אֵיזוֹ הֶאָרָה מֵאוֹר ה׳, וְלָכֵן נִקְרָא בְּשֵׁם ״זִיו הַשְּׁכִינָה״; אֲבָל הַקָּדוֹשׁ־בָּרוּךְ־הוּא בִּכְבוֹדוֹ וּבְעַצְמוֹ – לֵית מַחֲשָׁבָה תְּפִיסָא בֵיהּ כְּלָל, כִּי אִם כַּאֲשֶׁר תְּפִיסָא וּמִתְלַבֶּשֶׁת בַּתּוֹרָה וּמִצְוֹתֶיהָ, אֲזַי הִיא תְּפִיסָא וּמִתְלַבֶּשֶׁת בְּהַקָּדוֹשׁ־בָּרוּךְ־הוּא מַמָּשׁ, דְּאוֹרַיְיתָא וְקוּדְשָׁא־בְּרִיךְ־הוּא כּוּלָּא חַד.
sections.aramaic_translation: Hence it has been said: “Better is one hour of repentance and good deeds in this world than the whole life of the World to Come.”21 Avot 4:17. For, the World to Come is that state where one enjoys the effulgence of the Divine Presence,22 Berachot 16b. which is the pleasure of comprehension, yet no created being—even celestial—can comprehend more than some reflection of the Divine light; that is why the reference is to “effulgence of the Divine Presence” (Ziv haShechinah).23 I.e., the “glow” of the Shechinah, not the Shechinah itself. Note the author’s interpretation of זיו in this context as a remote gleam, a mere reflection. But as for the essence of the Holy One, blessed is He, no thought can apprehend Him at all, except when it apprehends, and is clothed in, the Torah and its mitzvot; only then does it truly apprehend, and is clothed in, the Holy One, blessed is He, inasmuch as the Torah and the Holy One, blessed is He, are one and the same.
sections.text: Hence it has been said: “Better is one hour of repentance and good deeds in this world than the whole life of the World to Come.”21 Avot 4:17. For, the World to Come is that state where one enjoys the effulgence of the Divine Presence,22 Berachot 16b. which is the pleasure of comprehension, yet no created being—even celestial—can comprehend more than some reflection of the Divine light; that is why the reference is to “effulgence of the Divine Presence” (Ziv haShechinah).23 I.e., the “glow” of the Shechinah, not the Shechinah itself. Note the author’s interpretation of זיו in this context as a remote gleam, a mere reflection. But as for the essence of the Holy One, blessed is He, no thought can apprehend Him at all, except when it apprehends, and is clothed in, the Torah and its mitzvot; only then does it truly apprehend, and is clothed in, the Holy One, blessed is He, inasmuch as the Torah and the Holy One, blessed is He, are one and the same.
sections.text_simple: Hence it has been said: “Better is one hour of repentance and good deeds in this world than the whole life of the World to Come.”21 Avot 4:17. For, the World to Come is that state where one enjoys the effulgence of the Divine Presence,22 Berachot 16b. which is the pleasure of comprehension, yet no created being—even celestial—can comprehend more than some reflection of the Divine light; that is why the reference is to “effulgence of the Divine Presence” (Ziv haShechinah).23 I.e., the “glow” of the Shechinah, not the Shechinah itself. Note the author’s interpretation of זיו in this context as a remote gleam, a mere reflection. But as for the essence of the Holy One, blessed is He, no thought can apprehend Him at all, except when it apprehends, and is clothed in, the Torah and its mitzvot; only then does it truly apprehend, and is clothed in, the Holy One, blessed is He, inasmuch as the Torah and the Holy One, blessed is He, are one and the same.

sections.aramaic: וְאַף שֶׁהַתּוֹרָה נִתְלַבְּשָׁה בִּדְבָרִים תַּחְתּוֹנִים גַּשְׁמִיִּים – הֲרֵי זֶה כִּמְחַבֵּק אֶת הַמֶּלֶךְ דֶּרֶךְ מָשָׁל, שֶׁאֵין הֶפְרֵשׁ בְּמַעֲלַת הִתְקָרְבוּתוֹ וּדְבֵיקוּתוֹ בַּמֶּלֶךְ, בֵּין מְחַבְּקוֹ כְּשֶׁהוּא לָבוּשׁ לְבוּשׁ אֶחָד בֵּין שֶׁהוּא לָבוּשׁ כַּמָּה לְבוּשִׁים, מֵאַחַר שֶׁגּוּף הַמֶּלֶךְ בְּתוֹכָם.
sections.aramaic_translation: For although the Torah has been clothed in lower material things, it is by way of illustration, like embracing the king. There is no difference, in regard to the degree of closeness and attachment to the king, whether while embracing the king, the latter is then wearing one robe or several robes, so long as the royal person is in them.
sections.text: For although the Torah has been clothed in lower material things, it is by way of illustration, like embracing the king. There is no difference, in regard to the degree of closeness and attachment to the king, whether while embracing the king, the latter is then wearing one robe or several robes, so long as the royal person is in them.
sections.text_simple: For although the Torah has been clothed in lower material things, it is by way of illustration, like embracing the king. There is no difference, in regard to the degree of closeness and attachment to the king, whether while embracing the king, the latter is then wearing one robe or several robes, so long as the royal person is in them.

sections.aramaic: וְכֵן אִם הַמֶּלֶךְ מְחַבְּקוֹ בִּזְרוֹעוֹ, גַּם שֶׁהִיא מְלוּבֶּשֶׁת תּוֹךְ מַלְבּוּשָׁיו, כְּמוֹ שֶׁכָּתוּב: ״וִימִינוֹ תְחַבְּקֵנִי״, שֶׁהִיא הַתּוֹרָה שֶׁנִּתְּנָה מִיָּמִין, שֶׁהִיא בְּחִינַת חֶסֶד וּמַיִם:
sections.aramaic_translation: Likewise, when the king, for his part, embraces one with his arm, even though it is dressed in his robes; as it is written, “And His right hand embraces me,”24 Song of Songs 8:3. which refers to the Torah which was given by G–d’s right hand,25 Deuteronomy 35:2. which is the quality of chesed and water.26 In Kabbalah, the “right” hand or side is the side of benevolence. Chesed and water are synonymous of Divine benevolence. See note 10, ch. 3.
sections.text: Likewise, when the king, for his part, embraces one with his arm, even though it is dressed in his robes; as it is written, “And His right hand embraces me,”24 Song of Songs 8:3. which refers to the Torah which was given by G–d’s right hand,25 Deuteronomy 35:2. which is the quality of chesed and water.26 In Kabbalah, the “right” hand or side is the side of benevolence. Chesed and water are synonymous of Divine benevolence. See note 10, ch. 3.
sections.text_simple: Likewise, when the king, for his part, embraces one with his arm, even though it is dressed in his robes; as it is written, “And His right hand embraces me,”24 Song of Songs 8:3. which refers to the Torah which was given by G–d’s right hand,25 Deuteronomy 35:2. which is the quality of chesed and water.26 In Kabbalah, the “right” hand or side is the side of benevolence. Chesed and water are synonymous of Divine benevolence. See note 10, ch. 3.

sections.aramaic: וּלְתוֹסֶפֶת בֵּיאוּר, בַּאֵר הֵיטֵב לְשׁוֹן ״תְּפִיסָא״ שֶׁאָמַר אֵלִיָּהוּ: ״לֵית מַחֲשָׁבָה תְּפִיסָא בָךְ כוּ׳״.
sections.aramaic_translation: Let us explain further and fully elucidate the expression tefisa (apprehension) in the words of Elijah, “No thought can apprehend You.”1 Tikkunei Zohar, Introduction 17a.
sections.text: Let us explain further and fully elucidate the expression tefisa (apprehension) in the words of Elijah, “No thought can apprehend You.”1 Tikkunei Zohar, Introduction 17a.
sections.text_simple: Let us explain further and fully elucidate the expression tefisa (apprehension) in the words of Elijah, “No thought can apprehend You.”1 Tikkunei Zohar, Introduction 17a.

sections.aramaic: הִנֵּה, כָּל שֵׂכֶל כְּשֶׁמַּשְׂכִּיל וּמַשִּׂיג בְּשִׂכְלוֹ אֵיזֶה מוּשְׂכָּל, הֲרֵי הַשֵּׂכֶל תּוֹפֵס אֶת הַמּוּשְׂכָּל וּמַקִּיפוֹ בְּשִׂכְלוֹ, וְהַמּוּשְׂכָּל נִתְפָּס וּמוּקָּף וּמְלוּבָּשׁ בְּתוֹךְ הַשֵּׂכֶל שֶׁהִשִּׂיגוֹ וְהִשְׂכִּילוֹ.
sections.aramaic_translation: Now, when an intellect conceives and comprehends a concept with its intellectual faculties, this intellect grasps the concept and encompasses it. This concept is [in turn] grasped, enveloped, and enclothed within that intellect which conceived and comprehended it.
sections.text: Now, when an intellect conceives and comprehends a concept with its intellectual faculties, this intellect grasps the concept and encompasses it. This concept is [in turn] grasped, enveloped, and enclothed within that intellect which conceived and comprehended it.
sections.text_simple: Now, when an intellect conceives and comprehends a concept with its intellectual faculties, this intellect grasps the concept and encompasses it. This concept is [in turn] grasped, enveloped, and enclothed within that intellect which conceived and comprehended it.

sections.aramaic: וְגַם הַשֵּׂכֶל מְלוּבָּשׁ בַּמּוּשְׂכָּל בְּשָׁעָה שֶׁמַּשִּׂיגוֹ וְתוֹפְסוֹ בְּשִׂכְלוֹ. דֶּרֶךְ מָשָׁל: כְּשֶׁאָדָם מֵבִין וּמַשִּׂיג אֵיזוֹ הֲלָכָה בְּמִשְׁנָה אוֹ בִּגְמָרָא לַאֲשׁוּרָהּ עַל בּוּרְיָהּ הֲרֵי שִׂכְלוֹ תּוֹפֵס וּמַקִּיף אוֹתָהּ, וְגַם שִׂכְלוֹ מְלוּבָּשׁ בָּהּ בְּאוֹתָהּ שָׁעָה.
sections.aramaic_translation: The mind, for its part, is also clothed in the concept at the time it comprehends and grasps it with the intellect.2 This point is demonstrated by the fact that when the mind is preoccupied with one thing, it cannot at the same time engage in another. For example, when a person understands and comprehends, fully and clearly, any halachah in the Mishnah or Gemara, his intellect grasps and encompasses it and, at the same time, is clothed in it.
sections.text: The mind, for its part, is also clothed in the concept at the time it comprehends and grasps it with the intellect.2 This point is demonstrated by the fact that when the mind is preoccupied with one thing, it cannot at the same time engage in another. For example, when a person understands and comprehends, fully and clearly, any halachah in the Mishnah or Gemara, his intellect grasps and encompasses it and, at the same time, is clothed in it.
sections.text_simple: The mind, for its part, is also clothed in the concept at the time it comprehends and grasps it with the intellect.2 This point is demonstrated by the fact that when the mind is preoccupied with one thing, it cannot at the same time engage in another. For example, when a person understands and comprehends, fully and clearly, any halachah in the Mishnah or Gemara, his intellect grasps and encompasses it and, at the same time, is clothed in it.

sections.aramaic: וְהִנֵּה, הֲלָכָה זוֹ, הִיא חָכְמָתוֹ וּרְצוֹנוֹ שֶׁל הַקָּדוֹשׁ־בָּרוּךְ־הוּא, שֶׁעָלָה בִּרְצוֹנוֹ, שֶׁכְּשֶׁיִּטְעוֹן רְאוּבֵן כָּךְ וְכָךְ דֶּרֶךְ מָשָׁל וְשִׁמְעוֹן כָּךְ וְכָךְ – יִהְיֶה הַפְּסַק בֵּינֵיהֶם כָּךְ וְכָךְ.
sections.aramaic_translation: Consequently, as the particular halachah is the wisdom and will of G–d, for it was His will that when, for example, Reuben pleads in one way and Simeon in another, the verdict as between them shall be thus and thus;
sections.text: Consequently, as the particular halachah is the wisdom and will of G–d, for it was His will that when, for example, Reuben pleads in one way and Simeon in another, the verdict as between them shall be thus and thus;
sections.text_simple: Consequently, as the particular halachah is the wisdom and will of G–d, for it was His will that when, for example, Reuben pleads in one way and Simeon in another, the verdict as between them shall be thus and thus;

sections.aramaic: וְאַף אִם לֹא הָיָה וְלֹא יִהְיֶה הַדָּבָר הַזֶּה לְעוֹלָם, לָבֹא לְמִשְׁפָּט עַל טְעָנוֹת וּתְבִיעוֹת אֵלּוּ, מִכָּל מָקוֹם, מֵאַחַר שֶׁכָּךְ עָלָה בִּרְצוֹנוֹ וְחָכְמָתוֹ שֶׁל הַקָּדוֹשׁ־בָּרוּךְ־הוּא, שֶׁאִם יִטְעוֹן זֶה כָּךְ וְזֶה כָּךְ, יִהְיֶה הַפְּסַק כָּךְ, הֲרֵי כְּשֶׁאָדָם יוֹדֵעַ וּמַשִּׂיג בְּשִׂכְלוֹ פְּסַק זֶה כַּהֲלָכָה הָעֲרוּכָה בְּמִשְׁנָה אוֹ גְּמָרָא אוֹ פּוֹסְקִים, הֲרֵי זֶה מַשִּׂיג וְתוֹפֵס וּמַקִּיף בְּשִׂכְלוֹ רְצוֹנוֹ וְחָכְמָתוֹ שֶׁל הַקָּדוֹשׁ־בָּרוּךְ־הוּא, דְּלֵית מַחֲשָׁבָה תְּפִיסָא בֵּיהּ וְלֹא בִּרְצוֹנוֹ וְחָכְמָתוֹ, כִּי אִם בְּהִתְלַבְּשׁוּתָם בַּהֲלָכוֹת הָעֲרוּכוֹת לְפָנֵינוּ, וְגַם שִׂכְלוֹ מְלוּבָּשׁ בָּהֶם.
sections.aramaic_translation: and even should such a litigation never have occurred, nor would it ever present itself for judgment in connection with such disputes and claims, nevertheless, since it has been the will and wisdom of the Holy One, blessed is He, that in the event of a person pleading this way and the other [litigant] pleading that way, the verdict shall be such and such—now therefore, when a person knows and comprehends with his intellect such a verdict in accordance with the law as it is set out in the Mishnah, Gemara, or Poskim (Codes), he has thus comprehended, grasped, and encompassed with his intellect the will and wisdom of the Holy One, blessed is He, Whom no thought can grasp, nor His will and wisdom, except when they are clothed in the laws that have been set out for us. [Simultaneously] the intellect is also clothed in them [the Divine will and wisdom].
sections.text: and even should such a litigation never have occurred, nor would it ever present itself for judgment in connection with such disputes and claims, nevertheless, since it has been the will and wisdom of the Holy One, blessed is He, that in the event of a person pleading this way and the other [litigant] pleading that way, the verdict shall be such and such—now therefore, when a person knows and comprehends with his intellect such a verdict in accordance with the law as it is set out in the Mishnah, Gemara, or Poskim (Codes), he has thus comprehended, grasped, and encompassed with his intellect the will and wisdom of the Holy One, blessed is He, Whom no thought can grasp, nor His will and wisdom, except when they are clothed in the laws that have been set out for us. [Simultaneously] the intellect is also clothed in them [the Divine will and wisdom].
sections.text_simple: and even should such a litigation never have occurred, nor would it ever present itself for judgment in connection with such disputes and claims, nevertheless, since it has been the will and wisdom of the Holy One, blessed is He, that in the event of a person pleading this way and the other [litigant] pleading that way, the verdict shall be such and such—now therefore, when a person knows and comprehends with his intellect such a verdict in accordance with the law as it is set out in the Mishnah, Gemara, or Poskim (Codes), he has thus comprehended, grasped, and encompassed with his intellect the will and wisdom of the Holy One, blessed is He, Whom no thought can grasp, nor His will and wisdom, except when they are clothed in the laws that have been set out for us. [Simultaneously] the intellect is also clothed in them [the Divine will and wisdom].

sections.aramaic: וְהוּא יִחוּד נִפְלָא שֶׁאֵין יִחוּד כָּמוֹהוּ וְלֹא כְּעֶרְכּוֹ נִמְצָא כְּלָל בְּגַשְׁמִיּוּת, לִהְיוֹת לַאֲחָדִים וּמְיוּחָדִים מַמָּשׁ מִכָּל צַד וּפִנָּה.
sections.aramaic_translation: This is a wonderful union, like which there is none other and which has no parallel anywhere in the material world, whereby complete oneness and unity, from every side and angle, could be attained.
sections.text: This is a wonderful union, like which there is none other and which has no parallel anywhere in the material world, whereby complete oneness and unity, from every side and angle, could be attained.
sections.text_simple: This is a wonderful union, like which there is none other and which has no parallel anywhere in the material world, whereby complete oneness and unity, from every side and angle, could be attained.

sections.aramaic: וְזֹאת מַעֲלָה יְתֵרָה גְּדוֹלָה וְנִפְלָאָה לְאֵין קֵץ אֲשֶׁר בְּמִצְוַת יְדִיעַת הַתּוֹרָה וְהַשָּׂגָתָהּ עַל כָּל הַמִּצְוֹת מַעֲשִׂיּוֹת, וַאֲפִילוּ עַל מִצְוֹת הַתְּלוּיוֹת בְּדִבּוּר, וַאֲפִילוּ עַל מִצְוַת תַּלְמוּד תּוֹרָה שֶׁבְּדִבּוּר.
sections.aramaic_translation: Hence the special superiority, infinitely great and wonderful, that is in the commandment of knowing the Torah and comprehending it, over all the commandments involving action, and even those relating to speech, and even the commandment to study the Torah,3 As distinct from knowledge of the Torah. which is fulfilled through speech.
sections.text: Hence the special superiority, infinitely great and wonderful, that is in the commandment of knowing the Torah and comprehending it, over all the commandments involving action, and even those relating to speech, and even the commandment to study the Torah,3 As distinct from knowledge of the Torah. which is fulfilled through speech.
sections.text_simple: Hence the special superiority, infinitely great and wonderful, that is in the commandment of knowing the Torah and comprehending it, over all the commandments involving action, and even those relating to speech, and even the commandment to study the Torah,3 As distinct from knowledge of the Torah. which is fulfilled through speech.

sections.aramaic: כִּי עַל יְדֵי כָּל הַמִּצְוֹת שֶׁבְּדִבּוּר וּמַעֲשֶׂה, הַקָּדוֹשׁ־בָּרוּךְ־הוּא מַלְבִּישׁ אֶת הַנֶּפֶשׁ, וּמַקִּיפָהּ אוֹר ה׳ מֵרֹאשָׁהּ וְעַד רַגְלָהּ, וּבִידִיעַת הַתּוֹרָה, מִלְּבַד שֶׁהַשֵּׂכֶל מְלוּבָּשׁ בְּחָכְמַת ה׳, הִנֵּה גַּם חָכְמַת ה׳ בְּקִרְבּוֹ – מַה שֶּׁהַשֵּׂכֶל מַשִּׂיג וְתוֹפֵס וּמַקִּיף בְּשִׂכְלוֹ מַה שֶּׁאֶפְשָׁר לוֹ לִתְפּוֹס וּלְהַשִּׂיג מִידִיעַת הַתּוֹרָה, אִישׁ כְּפִי שִׂכְלוֹ וְכֹחַ יְדִיעָתוֹ וְהַשָּׂגָתוֹ בִּפְשַׁט־רֶמֶז־דְּרוּשׁ־סוֹד.
sections.aramaic_translation: For, through all the commandments involving speech or action, the Holy One, blessed is He, clothes the soul and envelops it from head to foot with the Divine light. However, with regard to knowledge of the Torah, apart from the fact that the intellect is clothed in Divine wisdom, this Divine wisdom is also contained in it, to the extent that his intellect comprehends, grasps, and encompasses, as much as it is able so to do, of the knowledge of the Torah, every man according to his intellect, his knowledgeable capacity, and his comprehension in Pardes.4 Set ch. 4, note 4.
sections.text: For, through all the commandments involving speech or action, the Holy One, blessed is He, clothes the soul and envelops it from head to foot with the Divine light. However, with regard to knowledge of the Torah, apart from the fact that the intellect is clothed in Divine wisdom, this Divine wisdom is also contained in it, to the extent that his intellect comprehends, grasps, and encompasses, as much as it is able so to do, of the knowledge of the Torah, every man according to his intellect, his knowledgeable capacity, and his comprehension in Pardes.4 Set ch. 4, note 4.
sections.text_simple: For, through all the commandments involving speech or action, the Holy One, blessed is He, clothes the soul and envelops it from head to foot with the Divine light. However, with regard to knowledge of the Torah, apart from the fact that the intellect is clothed in Divine wisdom, this Divine wisdom is also contained in it, to the extent that his intellect comprehends, grasps, and encompasses, as much as it is able so to do, of the knowledge of the Torah, every man according to his intellect, his knowledgeable capacity, and his comprehension in Pardes.4 Set ch. 4, note 4.

sections.aramaic: וּלְפִי שֶׁבִּידִיעַת הַתּוֹרָה, הַתּוֹרָה מְלוּבֶּשֶׁת בְּנֶפֶשׁ הָאָדָם וְשִׂכְלוֹ וּמוּקֶּפֶת בְּתוֹכָם, לָכֵן נִקְרֵאת בְּשֵׁם ״לֶחֶם״ וּ״מְזוֹן הַנֶּפֶשׁ״.
sections.aramaic_translation: Since, in the case of knowledge of the Torah, the Torah is clothed in the soul and intellect of a person and is absorbed in them, it is called “bread” and “food” of the soul.
sections.text: Since, in the case of knowledge of the Torah, the Torah is clothed in the soul and intellect of a person and is absorbed in them, it is called “bread” and “food” of the soul.
sections.text_simple: Since, in the case of knowledge of the Torah, the Torah is clothed in the soul and intellect of a person and is absorbed in them, it is called “bread” and “food” of the soul.

sections.aramaic: כִּי כְּמוֹ שֶׁהַלֶּחֶם הַגַּשְׁמִי זָן אֶת הַגּוּף כְּשֶׁמַּכְנִיסוֹ בְּתוֹכוֹ וְקִרְבּוֹ מַמָּשׁ, וְנֶהְפָּךְ שָׁם לִהְיוֹת דָּם וּבָשָׂר כִּבְשָׂרוֹ, וַאֲזַי יִחְיֶה וְיִתְקַיֵּם, כָּךְ בִּידִיעַת הַתּוֹרָה וְהַשָּׂגָתָהּ בְּנֶפֶשׁ הָאָדָם שֶׁלּוֹמְדָהּ הֵיטֵב בְּעִיּוּן שִׂכְלוֹ עַד שֶׁנִּתְפֶּסֶת בְּשִׂכְלוֹ וּמִתְאַחֶדֶת עִמּוֹ וְהָיוּ לַאֲחָדִים – נַעֲשָׂה מָזוֹן לַנֶּפֶשׁ וְחַיִּים בְּקִרְבָּהּ, מֵחַיֵּי הַחַיִּים אֵין־סוֹף בָּרוּךְ־הוּא הַמְלוּבָּשׁ בְּחָכְמָתוֹ וְתוֹרָתוֹ שֶׁבְּקִרְבָּהּ.
sections.aramaic_translation: For just as physical bread nourishes the body as it is absorbed internally, in his very inner self, where it is transformed into blood and flesh of his flesh, whereby he lives and exists—so, too, it is with the knowledge of the Torah and its comprehension by the soul of the person who studies it well, with a concentration of his intellect, until the Torah is absorbed by his intellect and is united with it, and they become one. This becomes nourishment for the soul and its inner life from the Giver of life, the En Sof, blessed is He, Who is clothed in His wisdom and in His Torah that are [absorbed] in it [the soul].
sections.text: For just as physical bread nourishes the body as it is absorbed internally, in his very inner self, where it is transformed into blood and flesh of his flesh, whereby he lives and exists—so, too, it is with the knowledge of the Torah and its comprehension by the soul of the person who studies it well, with a concentration of his intellect, until the Torah is absorbed by his intellect and is united with it, and they become one. This becomes nourishment for the soul and its inner life from the Giver of life, the En Sof, blessed is He, Who is clothed in His wisdom and in His Torah that are [absorbed] in it [the soul].
sections.text_simple: For just as physical bread nourishes the body as it is absorbed internally, in his very inner self, where it is transformed into blood and flesh of his flesh, whereby he lives and exists—so, too, it is with the knowledge of the Torah and its comprehension by the soul of the person who studies it well, with a concentration of his intellect, until the Torah is absorbed by his intellect and is united with it, and they become one. This becomes nourishment for the soul and its inner life from the Giver of life, the En Sof, blessed is He, Who is clothed in His wisdom and in His Torah that are [absorbed] in it [the soul].

sections.aramaic: וְזֶהוּ שֶׁכָּתוּב: ״וְתוֹרָתְךָ בְּתוֹךְ מֵעָי״, וּכְמוֹ שֶׁכָּתוּב בְּ״עֵץ חַיִּים״ שַׁעַר מ״ד פֶּרֶק ג׳, שֶׁלְּבוּשֵׁי הַנְּשָׁמוֹת בְּגַן עֵדֶן הֵן הַמִּצְוֹת, וְהַתּוֹרָה הִיא הַמָּזוֹן לַנְּשָׁמוֹת שֶׁעָסְקוּ בָּעוֹלָם הַזֶּה בַּתּוֹרָה לִשְׁמָהּ, וּכְמוֹ שֶׁכָּתוּב בַּזֹּהַר וַיַּקְהֵל דַּף ר״י.
sections.aramaic_translation: This is the meaning of the verse, “And Your Torah is in my innards.”5 Psalms 40:9. It is also stated in Etz Chaim, Portal 44, ch. 3, that the “garments” of the soul in Gan Eden are the commandments while the Torah is the “food” for the souls which, during life on earth, had occupied themselves in the study of the Torah for its own sake. It is [similarly] written in the Zohar.6 II:210a ff.
sections.text: This is the meaning of the verse, “And Your Torah is in my innards.”5 Psalms 40:9. It is also stated in Etz Chaim, Portal 44, ch. 3, that the “garments” of the soul in Gan Eden are the commandments while the Torah is the “food” for the souls which, during life on earth, had occupied themselves in the study of the Torah for its own sake. It is [similarly] written in the Zohar.6 II:210a ff.
sections.text_simple: This is the meaning of the verse, “And Your Torah is in my innards.”5 Psalms 40:9. It is also stated in Etz Chaim, Portal 44, ch. 3, that the “garments” of the soul in Gan Eden are the commandments while the Torah is the “food” for the souls which, during life on earth, had occupied themselves in the study of the Torah for its own sake. It is [similarly] written in the Zohar.6 II:210a ff.

sections.aramaic: וְ״לִשְׁמָהּ״, הַיְינוּ כְּדֵי לְקַשֵּׁר נַפְשׁוֹ לַה׳ עַל יְדֵי הַשָּׂגַת הַתּוֹרָה, אִישׁ כְּפִי שִׂכְלוֹ, כְּמוֹ שֶׁכָּתוּב בִּ״פְרִי עֵץ חַיִּים״.
sections.aramaic_translation: As for the meaning of “for its own sake,”7 See below, chs. 39, 40, and 41, for a further elaboration. We have here a departure from the conventional concepts of lishemah and shelo lishemah. it is [study with the intent] to attach one’s soul to G–d through the comprehension of the Torah, each one according to his intellect, as explained in Pri Etz Chaim.
sections.text: As for the meaning of “for its own sake,”7 See below, chs. 39, 40, and 41, for a further elaboration. We have here a departure from the conventional concepts of lishemah and shelo lishemah. it is [study with the intent] to attach one’s soul to G–d through the comprehension of the Torah, each one according to his intellect, as explained in Pri Etz Chaim.
sections.text_simple: As for the meaning of “for its own sake,”7 See below, chs. 39, 40, and 41, for a further elaboration. We have here a departure from the conventional concepts of lishemah and shelo lishemah. it is [study with the intent] to attach one’s soul to G–d through the comprehension of the Torah, each one according to his intellect, as explained in Pri Etz Chaim.

sections.aramaic: [וְהַמָּזוֹן הִיא בְּחִינַת אוֹר פְּנִימִי, וְהַלְּבוּשִׁים בְּחִינַת מַקִּיפִים. וְלָכֵן אָמְרוּ רַבּוֹתֵינוּ־זִכְרוֹנָם־לִבְרָכָה, שֶׁתַּלְמוּד תּוֹרָה שָׁקוּל כְּנֶגֶד כָּל הַמִּצְוֹת, לְפִי שֶׁהַמִּצְוֹת הֵן לְבוּשִׁים לְבַד, וְהַתּוֹרָה הִיא מָזוֹן וְגַם לְבוּשׁ לַנֶּפֶשׁ הַמַּשְׂכֶּלֶת, שֶׁמִּתְלַבֵּשׁ בָּהּ בְּעִיּוּנָהּ וְלִמּוּדָהּ, וְכָל שֶׁכֵּן כְּשֶׁמּוֹצִיא בְּפִיו בְּדִבּוּר, שֶׁהֶבֶל הַדִּבּוּר נַעֲשֶׂה בְּחִינַת אוֹר מַקִּיף, כְּמוֹ שֶׁכָּתוּב בִּ״פְרִי עֵץ חַיִּים״]:
sections.aramaic_translation: [The “food” [of the soul] is in the nature of “inner light,” while the “garments” are in the nature of “encompassing light.” Therefore our Rabbis, of blessed memory, have said, “The study of the Torah is equivalent to them all.”8 Mishnah, Peah 1:1. For the commandments are but “garments” whereas the Torah is both “food” as well as “garment”9 The “food” would correspond to the knowledge absorbed and “digested”; the “garment”—to that knowledge which is not thoroughly assimilated and remains external as it were, yet retaining the quality of the Divine precept, like all other religious acts which are conceived as “garments” of the soul. for the rational soul, in which a person is clothed during learning and concentration. All the more so when a person also articulates, by word of mouth; for the breath emitted in speaking [the words of the Torah] becomes something in the nature of an “encompassing light,” as is explained in Pri Etz Chaim.]
sections.text: [The “food” [of the soul] is in the nature of “inner light,” while the “garments” are in the nature of “encompassing light.” Therefore our Rabbis, of blessed memory, have said, “The study of the Torah is equivalent to them all.”8 Mishnah, Peah 1:1. For the commandments are but “garments” whereas the Torah is both “food” as well as “garment”9 The “food” would correspond to the knowledge absorbed and “digested”; the “garment”—to that knowledge which is not thoroughly assimilated and remains external as it were, yet retaining the quality of the Divine precept, like all other religious acts which are conceived as “garments” of the soul. for the rational soul, in which a person is clothed during learning and concentration. All the more so when a person also articulates, by word of mouth; for the breath emitted in speaking [the words of the Torah] becomes something in the nature of an “encompassing light,” as is explained in Pri Etz Chaim.]
sections.text_simple: [The “food” [of the soul] is in the nature of “inner light,” while the “garments” are in the nature of “encompassing light.” Therefore our Rabbis, of blessed memory, have said, “The study of the Torah is equivalent to them all.”8 Mishnah, Peah 1:1. For the commandments are but “garments” whereas the Torah is both “food” as well as “garment”9 The “food” would correspond to the knowledge absorbed and “digested”; the “garment”—to that knowledge which is not thoroughly assimilated and remains external as it were, yet retaining the quality of the Divine precept, like all other religious acts which are conceived as “garments” of the soul. for the rational soul, in which a person is clothed during learning and concentration. All the more so when a person also articulates, by word of mouth; for the breath emitted in speaking [the words of the Torah] becomes something in the nature of an “encompassing light,” as is explained in Pri Etz Chaim.]

sections.aramaic: בְּרֵאשִׁ֖ית בָּרָ֣א אֱלֹהִ֑ים אֵ֥ת הַשָּׁמַ֖יִם וְאֵ֥ת הָאָֽרֶץ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: When God began to createaWhen God began to create In contrast to others “In the beginning God created.” heaven and earth—
sections.text: When God began to createaWhen God began to create In contrast to others “In the beginning God created.” heaven and earth—
sections.text_simple: When God began to createaWhen God began to create In contrast to others “In the beginning God created.” heaven and earth—

sections.aramaic: וְהָאָ֗רֶץ הָיְתָ֥ה תֹ֙הוּ֙ וָבֹ֔הוּ וְחֹ֖שֶׁךְ עַל־פְּנֵ֣י תְה֑וֹם וְר֣וּחַ אֱלֹהִ֔ים מְרַחֶ֖פֶת עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הַמָּֽיִם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: the earth being unformed and void, with darkness over the surface of the deep and a wind fromba wind from In contrast to others “the spirit of.” God sweeping over the water—
sections.text: the earth being unformed and void, with darkness over the surface of the deep and a wind fromba wind from In contrast to others “the spirit of.” God sweeping over the water—
sections.text_simple: the earth being unformed and void, with darkness over the surface of the deep and a wind fromba wind from In contrast to others “the spirit of.” God sweeping over the water—

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֖ים יְהִ֣י א֑וֹר וַֽיְהִי־אֽוֹר׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.
sections.text: God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.
sections.text_simple: God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּ֧רְא אֱלֹהִ֛ים אֶת־הָא֖וֹר כִּי־ט֑וֹב וַיַּבְדֵּ֣ל אֱלֹהִ֔ים בֵּ֥ין הָא֖וֹר וּבֵ֥ין הַחֹֽשֶׁךְ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God saw that the light was good, and God separated the light from the darkness.
sections.text: God saw that the light was good, and God separated the light from the darkness.
sections.text_simple: God saw that the light was good, and God separated the light from the darkness.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקְרָ֨א אֱלֹהִ֤ים ׀ לָאוֹר֙ י֔וֹם וְלַחֹ֖שֶׁךְ קָ֣רָא לָ֑יְלָה וַֽיְהִי־עֶ֥רֶב וַֽיְהִי־בֹ֖קֶר י֥וֹם אֶחָֽד׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: God called the light Day—and called the darkness Night. And there was evening and there was morning, a first day.ca first day In contrast to others “one day.”
sections.text: God called the light Day—and called the darkness Night. And there was evening and there was morning, a first day.ca first day In contrast to others “one day.”
sections.text_simple: God called the light Day—and called the darkness Night. And there was evening and there was morning, a first day.ca first day In contrast to others “one day.”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֔ים יְהִ֥י רָקִ֖יעַ בְּת֣וֹךְ הַמָּ֑יִם וִיהִ֣י מַבְדִּ֔יל בֵּ֥ין מַ֖יִם לָמָֽיִם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “Let there be an expanse in the midst of the water, that it may separate water from water.”
sections.text: God said, “Let there be an expanse in the midst of the water, that it may separate water from water.”
sections.text_simple: God said, “Let there be an expanse in the midst of the water, that it may separate water from water.”

sections.aramaic: וַיַּ֣עַשׂ אֱלֹהִים֮ אֶת־הָרָקִ֒יעַ֒ וַיַּבְדֵּ֗ל בֵּ֤ין הַמַּ֙יִם֙ אֲשֶׁר֙ מִתַּ֣חַת לָרָקִ֔יעַ וּבֵ֣ין הַמַּ֔יִם אֲשֶׁ֖ר מֵעַ֣ל לָרָקִ֑יעַ וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God made the expanse, and it separated the water that was below the expanse from the water that was above the expanse. And it was so.
sections.text: God made the expanse, and it separated the water that was below the expanse from the water that was above the expanse. And it was so.
sections.text_simple: God made the expanse, and it separated the water that was below the expanse from the water that was above the expanse. And it was so.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקְרָ֧א אֱלֹהִ֛ים לָֽרָקִ֖יעַ שָׁמָ֑יִם וַֽיְהִי־עֶ֥רֶב וַֽיְהִי־בֹ֖קֶר י֥וֹם שֵׁנִֽי׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: God called the expanse Sky. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.
sections.text: God called the expanse Sky. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.
sections.text_simple: God called the expanse Sky. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֗ים יִקָּו֨וּ הַמַּ֜יִם מִתַּ֤חַת הַשָּׁמַ֙יִם֙ אֶל־מָק֣וֹם אֶחָ֔ד וְתֵרָאֶ֖ה הַיַּבָּשָׁ֑ה וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “Let the water below the sky be gathered into one area, that the dry land may appear.” And it was so.
sections.text: God said, “Let the water below the sky be gathered into one area, that the dry land may appear.” And it was so.
sections.text_simple: God said, “Let the water below the sky be gathered into one area, that the dry land may appear.” And it was so.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקְרָ֨א אֱלֹהִ֤ים ׀ לַיַּבָּשָׁה֙ אֶ֔רֶץ וּלְמִקְוֵ֥ה הַמַּ֖יִם קָרָ֣א יַמִּ֑ים וַיַּ֥רְא אֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־טֽוֹב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God called the dry land Earth—and called the gathering of waters Seas. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text: God called the dry land Earth—and called the gathering of waters Seas. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text_simple: God called the dry land Earth—and called the gathering of waters Seas. And God saw that this was good.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֗ים תַּֽדְשֵׁ֤א הָאָ֙רֶץ֙ דֶּ֗שֶׁא עֵ֚שֶׂב מַזְרִ֣יעַ זֶ֔רַע עֵ֣ץ פְּרִ֞י עֹ֤שֶׂה פְּרִי֙ לְמִינ֔וֹ אֲשֶׁ֥ר זַרְעוֹ־ב֖וֹ עַל־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And God said, “Let the earth sprout vegetation: seed-bearing plants, fruit trees of every kind on earth that bear fruit with the seed in it.” And it was so.
sections.text: And God said, “Let the earth sprout vegetation: seed-bearing plants, fruit trees of every kind on earth that bear fruit with the seed in it.” And it was so.
sections.text_simple: And God said, “Let the earth sprout vegetation: seed-bearing plants, fruit trees of every kind on earth that bear fruit with the seed in it.” And it was so.

sections.aramaic: וַתּוֹצֵ֨א הָאָ֜רֶץ דֶּ֠שֶׁא עֵ֣שֶׂב מַזְרִ֤יעַ זֶ֙רַע֙ לְמִינֵ֔הוּ וְעֵ֧ץ עֹֽשֶׂה־פְּרִ֛י אֲשֶׁ֥ר זַרְעוֹ־ב֖וֹ לְמִינֵ֑הוּ וַיַּ֥רְא אֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־טֽוֹב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The earth brought forth vegetation: seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees of every kind bearing fruit with the seed in it. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text: The earth brought forth vegetation: seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees of every kind bearing fruit with the seed in it. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text_simple: The earth brought forth vegetation: seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees of every kind bearing fruit with the seed in it. And God saw that this was good.

sections.aramaic: וַֽיְהִי־עֶ֥רֶב וַֽיְהִי־בֹ֖קֶר י֥וֹם שְׁלִישִֽׁי׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: And there was evening and there was morning, a third day.
sections.text: And there was evening and there was morning, a third day.
sections.text_simple: And there was evening and there was morning, a third day.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֗ים יְהִ֤י מְאֹרֹת֙ בִּרְקִ֣יעַ הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם לְהַבְדִּ֕יל בֵּ֥ין הַיּ֖וֹם וּבֵ֣ין הַלָּ֑יְלָה וְהָי֤וּ לְאֹתֹת֙ וּלְמ֣וֹעֲדִ֔ים וּלְיָמִ֖ים וְשָׁנִֽים׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate day from night; they shall serve as signs for the set times—the days and the years;
sections.text: God said, “Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate day from night; they shall serve as signs for the set times—the days and the years;
sections.text_simple: God said, “Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate day from night; they shall serve as signs for the set times—the days and the years;

sections.aramaic: וְהָי֤וּ לִמְאוֹרֹת֙ בִּרְקִ֣יעַ הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם לְהָאִ֖יר עַל־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: and they shall serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth.” And it was so.
sections.text: and they shall serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth.” And it was so.
sections.text_simple: and they shall serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth.” And it was so.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּ֣עַשׂ אֱלֹהִ֔ים אֶת־שְׁנֵ֥י הַמְּאֹרֹ֖ת הַגְּדֹלִ֑ים אֶת־הַמָּא֤וֹר הַגָּדֹל֙ לְמֶמְשֶׁ֣לֶת הַיּ֔וֹם וְאֶת־הַמָּא֤וֹר הַקָּטֹן֙ לְמֶמְשֶׁ֣לֶת הַלַּ֔יְלָה וְאֵ֖ת הַכּוֹכָבִֽים׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God made the two great lights, the greater light to dominate the day and the lesser light to dominate the night, and the stars.
sections.text: God made the two great lights, the greater light to dominate the day and the lesser light to dominate the night, and the stars.
sections.text_simple: God made the two great lights, the greater light to dominate the day and the lesser light to dominate the night, and the stars.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּתֵּ֥ן אֹתָ֛ם אֱלֹהִ֖ים בִּרְקִ֣יעַ הַשָּׁמָ֑יִם לְהָאִ֖יר עַל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And God set them in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth,
sections.text: And God set them in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth,
sections.text_simple: And God set them in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth,

sections.aramaic: וְלִמְשֹׁל֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם וּבַלַּ֔יְלָה וּֽלְהַבְדִּ֔יל בֵּ֥ין הָא֖וֹר וּבֵ֣ין הַחֹ֑שֶׁךְ וַיַּ֥רְא אֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־טֽוֹב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: to dominate the day and the night, and to separate light from darkness. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text: to dominate the day and the night, and to separate light from darkness. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text_simple: to dominate the day and the night, and to separate light from darkness. And God saw that this was good.

sections.aramaic: וַֽיְהִי־עֶ֥רֶב וַֽיְהִי־בֹ֖קֶר י֥וֹם רְבִיעִֽי׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: And there was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.
sections.text: And there was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.
sections.text_simple: And there was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֔ים יִשְׁרְצ֣וּ הַמַּ֔יִם שֶׁ֖רֶץ נֶ֣פֶשׁ חַיָּ֑ה וְעוֹף֙ יְעוֹפֵ֣ף עַל־הָאָ֔רֶץ עַל־פְּנֵ֖י רְקִ֥יעַ הַשָּׁמָֽיִם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and birds that fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky.”
sections.text: God said, “Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and birds that fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky.”
sections.text_simple: God said, “Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and birds that fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky.”

sections.aramaic: וַיִּבְרָ֣א אֱלֹהִ֔ים אֶת־הַתַּנִּינִ֖ם הַגְּדֹלִ֑ים וְאֵ֣ת כׇּל־נֶ֣פֶשׁ הַֽחַיָּ֣ה ׀ הָֽרֹמֶ֡שֶׂת אֲשֶׁר֩ שָׁרְצ֨וּ הַמַּ֜יִם לְמִֽינֵהֶ֗ם וְאֵ֨ת כׇּל־ע֤וֹף כָּנָף֙ לְמִינֵ֔הוּ וַיַּ֥רְא אֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־טֽוֹב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God created the great sea monsters, and all the living creatures of every kind that creep, which the waters brought forth in swarms, and all the winged birds of every kind. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text: God created the great sea monsters, and all the living creatures of every kind that creep, which the waters brought forth in swarms, and all the winged birds of every kind. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text_simple: God created the great sea monsters, and all the living creatures of every kind that creep, which the waters brought forth in swarms, and all the winged birds of every kind. And God saw that this was good.

sections.aramaic: וַיְבָ֧רֶךְ אֹתָ֛ם אֱלֹהִ֖ים לֵאמֹ֑ר פְּר֣וּ וּרְב֗וּ וּמִלְא֤וּ אֶת־הַמַּ֙יִם֙ בַּיַּמִּ֔ים וְהָע֖וֹף יִ֥רֶב בָּאָֽרֶץ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God blessed them, saying, “Be fertile and increase, fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds increase on the earth.”
sections.text: God blessed them, saying, “Be fertile and increase, fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds increase on the earth.”
sections.text_simple: God blessed them, saying, “Be fertile and increase, fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds increase on the earth.”

sections.aramaic: וַֽיְהִי־עֶ֥רֶב וַֽיְהִי־בֹ֖קֶר י֥וֹם חֲמִישִֽׁי׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: And there was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.
sections.text: And there was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.
sections.text_simple: And there was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֗ים תּוֹצֵ֨א הָאָ֜רֶץ נֶ֤פֶשׁ חַיָּה֙ לְמִינָ֔הּ בְּהֵמָ֥ה וָרֶ֛מֶשׂ וְחַֽיְתוֹ־אֶ֖רֶץ לְמִינָ֑הּ וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “Let the earth bring forth every kind of living creature: cattle, creeping things, and wild beasts of every kind.” And it was so.
sections.text: God said, “Let the earth bring forth every kind of living creature: cattle, creeping things, and wild beasts of every kind.” And it was so.
sections.text_simple: God said, “Let the earth bring forth every kind of living creature: cattle, creeping things, and wild beasts of every kind.” And it was so.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּ֣עַשׂ אֱלֹהִים֩ אֶת־חַיַּ֨ת הָאָ֜רֶץ לְמִינָ֗הּ וְאֶת־הַבְּהֵמָה֙ לְמִינָ֔הּ וְאֵ֛ת כׇּל־רֶ֥מֶשׂ הָֽאֲדָמָ֖ה לְמִינֵ֑הוּ וַיַּ֥רְא אֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־טֽוֹב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God made wild beasts of every kind and cattle of every kind, and all kinds of creeping things of the earth. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text: God made wild beasts of every kind and cattle of every kind, and all kinds of creeping things of the earth. And God saw that this was good.
sections.text_simple: God made wild beasts of every kind and cattle of every kind, and all kinds of creeping things of the earth. And God saw that this was good.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֔ים נַֽעֲשֶׂ֥ה אָדָ֛ם בְּצַלְמֵ֖נוּ כִּדְמוּתֵ֑נוּ וְיִרְדּוּ֩ בִדְגַ֨ת הַיָּ֜ם וּבְע֣וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֗יִם וּבַבְּהֵמָה֙ וּבְכׇל־הָאָ֔רֶץ וּבְכׇל־הָרֶ֖מֶשׂ הָֽרֹמֵ֥שׂ עַל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And God said, “Let us make humankind in our image, after our likeness. They shall rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, the cattle, the whole earth, and all the creeping things that creep on earth.”
sections.text: And God said, “Let us make humankind in our image, after our likeness. They shall rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, the cattle, the whole earth, and all the creeping things that creep on earth.”
sections.text_simple: And God said, “Let us make humankind in our image, after our likeness. They shall rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, the cattle, the whole earth, and all the creeping things that creep on earth.”

sections.aramaic: וַיִּבְרָ֨א אֱלֹהִ֤ים ׀ אֶת־הָֽאָדָם֙ בְּצַלְמ֔וֹ בְּצֶ֥לֶם אֱלֹהִ֖ים בָּרָ֣א אֹת֑וֹ זָכָ֥ר וּנְקֵבָ֖ה בָּרָ֥א אֹתָֽם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And God created humankind in the divine image,creating it in the image of God—creating them male and female.
sections.text: And God created humankind in the divine image,creating it in the image of God—creating them male and female.
sections.text_simple: And God created humankind in the divine image,creating it in the image of God—creating them male and female.

sections.aramaic: וַיְבָ֣רֶךְ אֹתָם֮ אֱלֹהִים֒ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר לָהֶ֜ם אֱלֹהִ֗ים פְּר֥וּ וּרְב֛וּ וּמִלְא֥וּ אֶת־הָאָ֖רֶץ וְכִבְשֻׁ֑הָ וּרְד֞וּ בִּדְגַ֤ת הַיָּם֙ וּבְע֣וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וּבְכׇל־חַיָּ֖ה הָֽרֹמֶ֥שֶׂת עַל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God blessed them and God said to them, “Be fertile and increase, fill the earth and master it; and rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, and all the living things that creep on earth.”
sections.text: God blessed them and God said to them, “Be fertile and increase, fill the earth and master it; and rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, and all the living things that creep on earth.”
sections.text_simple: God blessed them and God said to them, “Be fertile and increase, fill the earth and master it; and rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, and all the living things that creep on earth.”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֗ים הִנֵּה֩ נָתַ֨תִּי לָכֶ֜ם אֶת־כׇּל־עֵ֣שֶׂב ׀ זֹרֵ֣עַ זֶ֗רַע אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל־פְּנֵ֣י כׇל־הָאָ֔רֶץ וְאֶת־כׇּל־הָעֵ֛ץ אֲשֶׁר־בּ֥וֹ פְרִי־עֵ֖ץ זֹרֵ֣עַ זָ֑רַע לָכֶ֥ם יִֽהְיֶ֖ה לְאׇכְלָֽה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: God said, “See, I give you every seed-bearing plant that is upon all the earth, and every tree that has seed-bearing fruit; they shall be yours for food.
sections.text: God said, “See, I give you every seed-bearing plant that is upon all the earth, and every tree that has seed-bearing fruit; they shall be yours for food.
sections.text_simple: God said, “See, I give you every seed-bearing plant that is upon all the earth, and every tree that has seed-bearing fruit; they shall be yours for food.

sections.aramaic: וּֽלְכׇל־חַיַּ֣ת הָ֠אָ֠רֶץ וּלְכׇל־ע֨וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֜יִם וּלְכֹ֣ל ׀ רוֹמֵ֣שׂ עַל־הָאָ֗רֶץ אֲשֶׁר־בּוֹ֙ נֶ֣פֶשׁ חַיָּ֔ה אֶת־כׇּל־יֶ֥רֶק עֵ֖שֶׂב לְאׇכְלָ֑ה וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And to all the animals on land, to all the birds of the sky, and to everything that creeps on earth, in which there is the breath of life, [I give] all the green plants for food.” And it was so.
sections.text: And to all the animals on land, to all the birds of the sky, and to everything that creeps on earth, in which there is the breath of life, [I give] all the green plants for food.” And it was so.
sections.text_simple: And to all the animals on land, to all the birds of the sky, and to everything that creeps on earth, in which there is the breath of life, [I give] all the green plants for food.” And it was so.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּ֤רְא אֱלֹהִים֙ אֶת־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֔ה וְהִנֵּה־ט֖וֹב מְאֹ֑ד וַֽיְהִי־עֶ֥רֶב וַֽיְהִי־בֹ֖קֶר י֥וֹם הַשִּׁשִּֽׁי׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: And God saw all that had been made, and found it very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
sections.text: And God saw all that had been made, and found it very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
sections.text_simple: And God saw all that had been made, and found it very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.

sections.aramaic: וַיְכֻלּ֛וּ הַשָּׁמַ֥יִם וְהָאָ֖רֶץ וְכׇל־צְבָאָֽם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The heaven and the earth were finished, and all their array.
sections.text: The heaven and the earth were finished, and all their array.
sections.text_simple: The heaven and the earth were finished, and all their array.

sections.aramaic: וַיְכַ֤ל אֱלֹהִים֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַשְּׁבִיעִ֔י מְלַאכְתּ֖וֹ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֑ה וַיִּשְׁבֹּת֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַשְּׁבִיעִ֔י מִכׇּל־מְלַאכְתּ֖וֹ אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָֽׂה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: On the seventh day God finished the work that had been undertaken, ceasingaceasing Or “resting.” on the seventh day from doing any of the work.
sections.text: On the seventh day God finished the work that had been undertaken, ceasingaceasing Or “resting.” on the seventh day from doing any of the work.
sections.text_simple: On the seventh day God finished the work that had been undertaken, ceasingaceasing Or “resting.” on the seventh day from doing any of the work.

sections.aramaic: וַיְבָ֤רֶךְ אֱלֹהִים֙ אֶת־י֣וֹם הַשְּׁבִיעִ֔י וַיְקַדֵּ֖שׁ אֹת֑וֹ כִּ֣י ב֤וֹ שָׁבַת֙ מִכׇּל־מְלַאכְתּ֔וֹ אֲשֶׁר־בָּרָ֥א אֱלֹהִ֖ים לַעֲשֽׂוֹת׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: And God blessed the seventh day and declared it holy—having ceased on it from all the work of creation that God had done.
sections.text: And God blessed the seventh day and declared it holy—having ceased on it from all the work of creation that God had done.
sections.text_simple: And God blessed the seventh day and declared it holy—having ceased on it from all the work of creation that God had done.

sections.aramaic: אֵ֣לֶּה תוֹלְד֧וֹת הַשָּׁמַ֛יִם וְהָאָ֖רֶץ בְּהִבָּֽרְאָ֑ם בְּי֗וֹם עֲשׂ֛וֹת יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶ֥רֶץ וְשָׁמָֽיִם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Such is the story of heaven and earth when they were created.When the ETERNAL God made earth and heaven—
sections.text: Such is the story of heaven and earth when they were created.When the ETERNAL God made earth and heaven—
sections.text_simple: Such is the story of heaven and earth when they were created.When the ETERNAL God made earth and heaven—

sections.aramaic: וְכֹ֣ל ׀ שִׂ֣יחַ הַשָּׂדֶ֗ה טֶ֚רֶם יִֽהְיֶ֣ה בָאָ֔רֶץ וְכׇל־עֵ֥שֶׂב הַשָּׂדֶ֖ה טֶ֣רֶם יִצְמָ֑ח כִּי֩ לֹ֨א הִמְטִ֜יר יְהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהִים֙ עַל־הָאָ֔רֶץ וְאָדָ֣ם אַ֔יִן לַֽעֲבֹ֖ד אֶת־הָֽאֲדָמָֽה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: when no shrub of the fieldbshrub of the field I.e., suitable for pasturage. was yet on earth and no grasses of the fieldcgrasses of the field I.e., cereal grasses, suitable as crops. had yet sprouted, because the ETERNAL God had not sent rain upon the earth and there were no human beings to till the soil,
sections.text: when no shrub of the fieldbshrub of the field I.e., suitable for pasturage. was yet on earth and no grasses of the fieldcgrasses of the field I.e., cereal grasses, suitable as crops. had yet sprouted, because the ETERNAL God had not sent rain upon the earth and there were no human beings to till the soil,
sections.text_simple: when no shrub of the fieldbshrub of the field I.e., suitable for pasturage. was yet on earth and no grasses of the fieldcgrasses of the field I.e., cereal grasses, suitable as crops. had yet sprouted, because the ETERNAL God had not sent rain upon the earth and there were no human beings to till the soil,

sections.aramaic: וְאֵ֖ד יַֽעֲלֶ֣ה מִן־הָאָ֑רֶץ וְהִשְׁקָ֖ה אֶֽת־כׇּל־פְּנֵ֥י הָֽאֲדָמָֽה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: but a flow would well up from the ground and water the whole surface of the earth—
sections.text: but a flow would well up from the ground and water the whole surface of the earth—
sections.text_simple: but a flow would well up from the ground and water the whole surface of the earth—

sections.aramaic: וַיִּ֩יצֶר֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֜ים אֶת־הָֽאָדָ֗ם עָפָר֙ מִן־הָ֣אֲדָמָ֔ה וַיִּפַּ֥ח בְּאַפָּ֖יו נִשְׁמַ֣ת חַיִּ֑ים וַיְהִ֥י הָֽאָדָ֖ם לְנֶ֥פֶשׁ חַיָּֽה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: the ETERNAL God formed a Humanda Human Heb. ha-ʼadam. Although this term is not gendered, to the ancient audience it went without saying that the progenitor of all human lineages was a male. from the soil’s humus,efrom the soil’s humus Heb. ʻaphar min ha-ʼadamah. blowing into his nostrils the breath of life: the Human became a living being.
sections.text: the ETERNAL God formed a Humanda Human Heb. ha-ʼadam. Although this term is not gendered, to the ancient audience it went without saying that the progenitor of all human lineages was a male. from the soil’s humus,efrom the soil’s humus Heb. ʻaphar min ha-ʼadamah. blowing into his nostrils the breath of life: the Human became a living being.
sections.text_simple: the ETERNAL God formed a Humanda Human Heb. ha-ʼadam. Although this term is not gendered, to the ancient audience it went without saying that the progenitor of all human lineages was a male. from the soil’s humus,efrom the soil’s humus Heb. ʻaphar min ha-ʼadamah. blowing into his nostrils the breath of life: the Human became a living being.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּטַּ֞ע יְהֹוָ֧ה אֱלֹהִ֛ים גַּן־בְּעֵ֖דֶן מִקֶּ֑דֶם וַיָּ֣שֶׂם שָׁ֔ם אֶת־הָֽאָדָ֖ם אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָצָֽר׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The ETERNAL God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and placed there the Human who had been fashioned.
sections.text: The ETERNAL God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and placed there the Human who had been fashioned.
sections.text_simple: The ETERNAL God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and placed there the Human who had been fashioned.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּצְמַ֞ח יְהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהִים֙ מִן־הָ֣אֲדָמָ֔ה כׇּל־עֵ֛ץ נֶחְמָ֥ד לְמַרְאֶ֖ה וְט֣וֹב לְמַאֲכָ֑ל וְעֵ֤ץ הַֽחַיִּים֙ בְּת֣וֹךְ הַגָּ֔ן וְעֵ֕ץ הַדַּ֖עַת ט֥וֹב וָרָֽע׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And from the ground the ETERNAL God caused to grow every tree that was pleasing to the sight and good for food, with the tree of life in the middle of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and bad.
sections.text: And from the ground the ETERNAL God caused to grow every tree that was pleasing to the sight and good for food, with the tree of life in the middle of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and bad.
sections.text_simple: And from the ground the ETERNAL God caused to grow every tree that was pleasing to the sight and good for food, with the tree of life in the middle of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and bad.

sections.aramaic: וְנָהָר֙ יֹצֵ֣א מֵעֵ֔דֶן לְהַשְׁק֖וֹת אֶת־הַגָּ֑ן וּמִשָּׁם֙ יִפָּרֵ֔ד וְהָיָ֖ה לְאַרְבָּעָ֥ה רָאשִֽׁים׃
sections.aramaic_translation: A river issues from Eden to water the garden, and it then divides and becomes four branches.
sections.text: A river issues from Eden to water the garden, and it then divides and becomes four branches.
sections.text_simple: A river issues from Eden to water the garden, and it then divides and becomes four branches.

sections.aramaic: שֵׁ֥ם הָֽאֶחָ֖ד פִּישׁ֑וֹן ה֣וּא הַסֹּבֵ֗ב אֵ֚ת כׇּל־אֶ֣רֶץ הַֽחֲוִילָ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־שָׁ֖ם הַזָּהָֽב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The name of the first is Pishon, the one that winds through the whole land of Havilah, where the gold is.
sections.text: The name of the first is Pishon, the one that winds through the whole land of Havilah, where the gold is.
sections.text_simple: The name of the first is Pishon, the one that winds through the whole land of Havilah, where the gold is.

sections.aramaic: וּֽזְהַ֛ב הָאָ֥רֶץ הַהִ֖וא ט֑וֹב שָׁ֥ם הַבְּדֹ֖לַח וְאֶ֥בֶן הַשֹּֽׁהַם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: (The gold of that land is good; bdellium is there, and lapis lazuli.flapis lazuli In contrast to others “onyx”; meaning of Heb. shoham uncertain.)
sections.text: (The gold of that land is good; bdellium is there, and lapis lazuli.flapis lazuli In contrast to others “onyx”; meaning of Heb. shoham uncertain.)
sections.text_simple: (The gold of that land is good; bdellium is there, and lapis lazuli.flapis lazuli In contrast to others “onyx”; meaning of Heb. shoham uncertain.)

sections.aramaic: וְשֵֽׁם־הַנָּהָ֥ר הַשֵּׁנִ֖י גִּיח֑וֹן ה֣וּא הַסּוֹבֵ֔ב אֵ֖ת כׇּל־אֶ֥רֶץ כּֽוּשׁ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The name of the second river is Gihon, the one that winds through the whole land of Cush.
sections.text: The name of the second river is Gihon, the one that winds through the whole land of Cush.
sections.text_simple: The name of the second river is Gihon, the one that winds through the whole land of Cush.

sections.aramaic: וְשֵׁ֨ם הַנָּהָ֤ר הַשְּׁלִישִׁי֙ חִדֶּ֔קֶל ה֥וּא הַֽהֹלֵ֖ךְ קִדְמַ֣ת אַשּׁ֑וּר וְהַנָּהָ֥ר הָֽרְבִיעִ֖י ה֥וּא פְרָֽת׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The name of the third river is Tigris, the one that flows east of Asshur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
sections.text: The name of the third river is Tigris, the one that flows east of Asshur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
sections.text_simple: The name of the third river is Tigris, the one that flows east of Asshur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקַּ֛ח יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת־הָֽאָדָ֑ם וַיַּנִּחֵ֣הוּ בְגַן־עֵ֔דֶן לְעׇבְדָ֖הּ וּלְשׇׁמְרָֽהּ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The ETERNAL God settled the Human in the garden of Eden, to till it and tend it.
sections.text: The ETERNAL God settled the Human in the garden of Eden, to till it and tend it.
sections.text_simple: The ETERNAL God settled the Human in the garden of Eden, to till it and tend it.

sections.aramaic: וַיְצַו֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֔ים עַל־הָֽאָדָ֖ם לֵאמֹ֑ר מִכֹּ֥ל עֵֽץ־הַגָּ֖ן אָכֹ֥ל תֹּאכֵֽל׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the ETERNAL God commanded the Human, saying, “Of every tree of the garden you are free to eat;
sections.text: And the ETERNAL God commanded the Human, saying, “Of every tree of the garden you are free to eat;
sections.text_simple: And the ETERNAL God commanded the Human, saying, “Of every tree of the garden you are free to eat;

sections.aramaic: וּמֵעֵ֗ץ הַדַּ֙עַת֙ ט֣וֹב וָרָ֔ע לֹ֥א תֹאכַ֖ל מִמֶּ֑נּוּ כִּ֗י בְּי֛וֹם אֲכׇלְךָ֥ מִמֶּ֖נּוּ מ֥וֹת תָּמֽוּת׃
sections.aramaic_translation: but as for the tree of knowledge of good and bad, you must not eat of it; for as soon as you eat of it, you shall die.”
sections.text: but as for the tree of knowledge of good and bad, you must not eat of it; for as soon as you eat of it, you shall die.”
sections.text_simple: but as for the tree of knowledge of good and bad, you must not eat of it; for as soon as you eat of it, you shall die.”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֔ים לֹא־ט֛וֹב הֱי֥וֹת הָֽאָדָ֖ם לְבַדּ֑וֹ אֶֽעֱשֶׂה־לּ֥וֹ עֵ֖זֶר כְּנֶגְדּֽוֹ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The ETERNAL God said, “It is not good for the Human to be alone; I will make a fitting counterpart for him.”
sections.text: The ETERNAL God said, “It is not good for the Human to be alone; I will make a fitting counterpart for him.”
sections.text_simple: The ETERNAL God said, “It is not good for the Human to be alone; I will make a fitting counterpart for him.”

sections.aramaic: וַיִּ֩צֶר֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֜ים מִן־הָֽאֲדָמָ֗ה כׇּל־חַיַּ֤ת הַשָּׂדֶה֙ וְאֵת֙ כׇּל־ע֣וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וַיָּבֵא֙ אֶל־הָ֣אָדָ֔ם לִרְא֖וֹת מַה־יִּקְרָא־ל֑וֹ וְכֹל֩ אֲשֶׁ֨ר יִקְרָא־ל֧וֹ הָֽאָדָ֛ם נֶ֥פֶשׁ חַיָּ֖ה ה֥וּא שְׁמֽוֹ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the ETERNAL God formed out of the earth all the wild beasts and all the birds of the sky, and brought them to the Human to see what he would call them; and whatever the Human called each living creature, that would be its name.
sections.text: And the ETERNAL God formed out of the earth all the wild beasts and all the birds of the sky, and brought them to the Human to see what he would call them; and whatever the Human called each living creature, that would be its name.
sections.text_simple: And the ETERNAL God formed out of the earth all the wild beasts and all the birds of the sky, and brought them to the Human to see what he would call them; and whatever the Human called each living creature, that would be its name.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקְרָ֨א הָֽאָדָ֜ם שֵׁמ֗וֹת לְכׇל־הַבְּהֵמָה֙ וּלְע֣וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וּלְכֹ֖ל חַיַּ֣ת הַשָּׂדֶ֑ה וּלְאָדָ֕ם לֹֽא־מָצָ֥א עֵ֖זֶר כְּנֶגְדּֽוֹ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the Human gave names to all the cattle and to the birds of the sky and to all the wild beasts; but no fitting counterpart for a human being was found.
sections.text: And the Human gave names to all the cattle and to the birds of the sky and to all the wild beasts; but no fitting counterpart for a human being was found.
sections.text_simple: And the Human gave names to all the cattle and to the birds of the sky and to all the wild beasts; but no fitting counterpart for a human being was found.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּפֵּל֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֧ים ׀ תַּרְדֵּמָ֛ה עַל־הָאָדָ֖ם וַיִּישָׁ֑ן וַיִּקַּ֗ח אַחַת֙ מִצַּלְעֹתָ֔יו וַיִּסְגֹּ֥ר בָּשָׂ֖ר תַּחְתֶּֽנָּה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: So the ETERNAL God cast a deep sleep upon the Human; and, while he slept, [God] took one of his sidesgsides So Septuagint and Ibn Ezra; trad. “ribs.” and closed up the flesh at that site.
sections.text: So the ETERNAL God cast a deep sleep upon the Human; and, while he slept, [God] took one of his sidesgsides So Septuagint and Ibn Ezra; trad. “ribs.” and closed up the flesh at that site.
sections.text_simple: So the ETERNAL God cast a deep sleep upon the Human; and, while he slept, [God] took one of his sidesgsides So Septuagint and Ibn Ezra; trad. “ribs.” and closed up the flesh at that site.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּ֩בֶן֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֧ים ׀ אֶֽת־הַצֵּלָ֛ע אֲשֶׁר־לָקַ֥ח מִן־הָֽאָדָ֖ם לְאִשָּׁ֑ה וַיְבִאֶ֖הָ אֶל־הָֽאָדָֽם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the ETERNAL God fashioned the side that had been taken from the Human into a woman, bringing her to the Human.
sections.text: And the ETERNAL God fashioned the side that had been taken from the Human into a woman, bringing her to the Human.
sections.text_simple: And the ETERNAL God fashioned the side that had been taken from the Human into a woman, bringing her to the Human.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֘אמֶר֮ הָֽאָדָם֒ זֹ֣את הַפַּ֗עַם עֶ֚צֶם מֵֽעֲצָמַ֔י וּבָשָׂ֖ר מִבְּשָׂרִ֑י לְזֹאת֙ יִקָּרֵ֣א אִשָּׁ֔ה כִּ֥י מֵאִ֖ישׁ לֻֽקְחָה־זֹּֽאת׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Then the Human said,“This one at lastIs bone of my bonesAnd flesh of my flesh.This one shall be called the Woman,hthe Woman Heb. ʼishshah; i.e., the unique female participant in the situation. For from the Manithe Man Heb. ʼish; i.e., the sole (and male) participant in the original situation. was she taken.”
sections.text: Then the Human said,“This one at lastIs bone of my bonesAnd flesh of my flesh.This one shall be called the Woman,hthe Woman Heb. ʼishshah; i.e., the unique female participant in the situation. For from the Manithe Man Heb. ʼish; i.e., the sole (and male) participant in the original situation. was she taken.”
sections.text_simple: Then the Human said,“This one at lastIs bone of my bonesAnd flesh of my flesh.This one shall be called the Woman,hthe Woman Heb. ʼishshah; i.e., the unique female participant in the situation. For from the Manithe Man Heb. ʼish; i.e., the sole (and male) participant in the original situation. was she taken.”

sections.aramaic: עַל־כֵּן֙ יַֽעֲזׇב־אִ֔ישׁ אֶת־אָבִ֖יו וְאֶת־אִמּ֑וֹ וְדָבַ֣ק בְּאִשְׁתּ֔וֹ וְהָי֖וּ לְבָשָׂ֥ר אֶחָֽד׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Hence a manja man Heb. ʼish. leaves his father and mother and clings to his wife,khis wife Heb. ʼishto, from ʼishshah. so that they become one flesh.
sections.text: Hence a manja man Heb. ʼish. leaves his father and mother and clings to his wife,khis wife Heb. ʼishto, from ʼishshah. so that they become one flesh.
sections.text_simple: Hence a manja man Heb. ʼish. leaves his father and mother and clings to his wife,khis wife Heb. ʼishto, from ʼishshah. so that they become one flesh.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּֽהְי֤וּ שְׁנֵיהֶם֙ עֲרוּמִּ֔ים הָֽאָדָ֖ם וְאִשְׁתּ֑וֹ וְלֹ֖א יִתְבֹּשָֽׁשׁוּ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The two of them were naked,lnaked Heb. ʻarummim, play on ʻarum “shrewd” in 3.1. the Human and his wife, yet they felt no shame.
sections.text: The two of them were naked,lnaked Heb. ʻarummim, play on ʻarum “shrewd” in 3.1. the Human and his wife, yet they felt no shame.
sections.text_simple: The two of them were naked,lnaked Heb. ʻarummim, play on ʻarum “shrewd” in 3.1. the Human and his wife, yet they felt no shame.

sections.aramaic: וְהַנָּחָשׁ֙ הָיָ֣ה עָר֔וּם מִכֹּל֙ חַיַּ֣ת הַשָּׂדֶ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָׂ֖ה יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֑ים וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־הָ֣אִשָּׁ֔ה אַ֚ף כִּֽי־אָמַ֣ר אֱלֹהִ֔ים לֹ֣א תֹֽאכְל֔וּ מִכֹּ֖ל עֵ֥ץ הַגָּֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Now the serpent was the shrewdest of all the wild beasts that the ETERNAL God had made. It said to the woman, “Did God really say: You shall not eat of any tree of the garden?”
sections.text: Now the serpent was the shrewdest of all the wild beasts that the ETERNAL God had made. It said to the woman, “Did God really say: You shall not eat of any tree of the garden?”
sections.text_simple: Now the serpent was the shrewdest of all the wild beasts that the ETERNAL God had made. It said to the woman, “Did God really say: You shall not eat of any tree of the garden?”

sections.aramaic: וַתֹּ֥אמֶר הָֽאִשָּׁ֖ה אֶל־הַנָּחָ֑שׁ מִפְּרִ֥י עֵֽץ־הַגָּ֖ן נֹאכֵֽל׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The woman replied to the serpent, “We may eat of the fruit of the other trees of the garden.
sections.text: The woman replied to the serpent, “We may eat of the fruit of the other trees of the garden.
sections.text_simple: The woman replied to the serpent, “We may eat of the fruit of the other trees of the garden.

sections.aramaic: וּמִפְּרִ֣י הָעֵץ֮ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּתוֹךְ־הַגָּן֒ אָמַ֣ר אֱלֹהִ֗ים לֹ֤א תֹֽאכְלוּ֙ מִמֶּ֔נּוּ וְלֹ֥א תִגְּע֖וּ בּ֑וֹ פֶּן־תְּמֻתֽוּן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: It is only about fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden that God said: ‘You shall not eat of it or touch it, lest you die.’”
sections.text: It is only about fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden that God said: ‘You shall not eat of it or touch it, lest you die.’”
sections.text_simple: It is only about fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden that God said: ‘You shall not eat of it or touch it, lest you die.’”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר הַנָּחָ֖שׁ אֶל־הָֽאִשָּׁ֑ה לֹֽא־מ֖וֹת תְּמֻתֽוּן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the serpent said to the woman, “You are not going to die,
sections.text: And the serpent said to the woman, “You are not going to die,
sections.text_simple: And the serpent said to the woman, “You are not going to die,

sections.aramaic: כִּ֚י יֹדֵ֣עַ אֱלֹהִ֔ים כִּ֗י בְּיוֹם֙ אֲכׇלְכֶ֣ם מִמֶּ֔נּוּ וְנִפְקְח֖וּ עֵֽינֵיכֶ֑ם וִהְיִיתֶם֙ כֵּֽאלֹהִ֔ים יֹדְעֵ֖י ט֥וֹב וָרָֽע׃
sections.aramaic_translation: but God knows that as soon as you eat of it your eyes will be opened and you will be like divine beings who knowadivine beings who know In contrast to others “God, who knows.” good and bad.”
sections.text: but God knows that as soon as you eat of it your eyes will be opened and you will be like divine beings who knowadivine beings who know In contrast to others “God, who knows.” good and bad.”
sections.text_simple: but God knows that as soon as you eat of it your eyes will be opened and you will be like divine beings who knowadivine beings who know In contrast to others “God, who knows.” good and bad.”

sections.aramaic: וַתֵּ֣רֶא הָֽאִשָּׁ֡ה כִּ֣י טוֹב֩ הָעֵ֨ץ לְמַאֲכָ֜ל וְכִ֧י תַֽאֲוָה־ה֣וּא לָעֵינַ֗יִם וְנֶחְמָ֤ד הָעֵץ֙ לְהַשְׂכִּ֔יל וַתִּקַּ֥ח מִפִּרְי֖וֹ וַתֹּאכַ֑ל וַתִּתֵּ֧ן גַּם־לְאִישָׁ֛הּ עִמָּ֖הּ וַיֹּאכַֽל׃
sections.aramaic_translation: When the woman saw that the tree was good for eating and a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was desirable as a source of wisdom, she took of its fruit and ate. She also gave some to her husband, and he ate.
sections.text: When the woman saw that the tree was good for eating and a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was desirable as a source of wisdom, she took of its fruit and ate. She also gave some to her husband, and he ate.
sections.text_simple: When the woman saw that the tree was good for eating and a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was desirable as a source of wisdom, she took of its fruit and ate. She also gave some to her husband, and he ate.

sections.aramaic: וַתִּפָּקַ֙חְנָה֙ עֵינֵ֣י שְׁנֵיהֶ֔ם וַיֵּ֣דְע֔וּ כִּ֥י עֵֽירֻמִּ֖ם הֵ֑ם וַֽיִּתְפְּרוּ֙ עֲלֵ֣ה תְאֵנָ֔ה וַיַּעֲשׂ֥וּ לָהֶ֖ם חֲגֹרֹֽת׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Then the eyes of both of them were opened and they perceived that they were naked; and they sewed together fig leaves and made themselves loincloths.
sections.text: Then the eyes of both of them were opened and they perceived that they were naked; and they sewed together fig leaves and made themselves loincloths.
sections.text_simple: Then the eyes of both of them were opened and they perceived that they were naked; and they sewed together fig leaves and made themselves loincloths.

sections.aramaic: וַֽיִּשְׁמְע֞וּ אֶת־ק֨וֹל יְהֹוָ֧ה אֱלֹהִ֛ים מִתְהַלֵּ֥ךְ בַּגָּ֖ן לְר֣וּחַ הַיּ֑וֹם וַיִּתְחַבֵּ֨א הָֽאָדָ֜ם וְאִשְׁתּ֗וֹ מִפְּנֵי֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֔ים בְּת֖וֹךְ עֵ֥ץ הַגָּֽן׃
sections.aramaic_translation: They heard the sound of the ETERNAL God moving about in the garden at the breezy time of day; and the Human and his wife hid from the ETERNAL God among the trees of the garden.
sections.text: They heard the sound of the ETERNAL God moving about in the garden at the breezy time of day; and the Human and his wife hid from the ETERNAL God among the trees of the garden.
sections.text_simple: They heard the sound of the ETERNAL God moving about in the garden at the breezy time of day; and the Human and his wife hid from the ETERNAL God among the trees of the garden.

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקְרָ֛א יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶל־הָֽאָדָ֑ם וַיֹּ֥אמֶר ל֖וֹ אַיֶּֽכָּה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The ETERNAL God called out to the Human and said to him, “Where are you?”
sections.text: The ETERNAL God called out to the Human and said to him, “Where are you?”
sections.text_simple: The ETERNAL God called out to the Human and said to him, “Where are you?”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר אֶת־קֹלְךָ֥ שָׁמַ֖עְתִּי בַּגָּ֑ן וָאִירָ֛א כִּֽי־עֵירֹ֥ם אָנֹ֖כִי וָאֵחָבֵֽא׃
sections.aramaic_translation: He replied, “I heard the sound of You in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked, so I hid.”
sections.text: He replied, “I heard the sound of You in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked, so I hid.”
sections.text_simple: He replied, “I heard the sound of You in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked, so I hid.”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מִ֚י הִגִּ֣יד לְךָ֔ כִּ֥י עֵירֹ֖ם אָ֑תָּה הֲמִן־הָעֵ֗ץ אֲשֶׁ֧ר צִוִּיתִ֛יךָ לְבִלְתִּ֥י אֲכׇל־מִמֶּ֖נּוּ אָכָֽלְתָּ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: “Who told you that you were naked? Did you eat of the tree from which I had forbidden you to eat?”
sections.text: “Who told you that you were naked? Did you eat of the tree from which I had forbidden you to eat?”
sections.text_simple: “Who told you that you were naked? Did you eat of the tree from which I had forbidden you to eat?”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר הָֽאָדָ֑ם הָֽאִשָּׁה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר נָתַ֣תָּה עִמָּדִ֔י הִ֛וא נָֽתְנָה־לִּ֥י מִן־הָעֵ֖ץ וָאֹכֵֽל׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The Human said, “The woman You put at my side—she gave me of the tree, and I ate.”
sections.text: The Human said, “The woman You put at my side—she gave me of the tree, and I ate.”
sections.text_simple: The Human said, “The woman You put at my side—she gave me of the tree, and I ate.”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֧ה אֱלֹהִ֛ים לָאִשָּׁ֖ה מַה־זֹּ֣את עָשִׂ֑ית וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ הָֽאִשָּׁ֔ה הַנָּחָ֥שׁ הִשִּׁיאַ֖נִי וָאֹכֵֽל׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the ETERNAL God said to the woman, “What is this you have done!” The woman replied, “The serpent duped me, and I ate.”
sections.text: And the ETERNAL God said to the woman, “What is this you have done!” The woman replied, “The serpent duped me, and I ate.”
sections.text_simple: And the ETERNAL God said to the woman, “What is this you have done!” The woman replied, “The serpent duped me, and I ate.”

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֥ים ׀ אֶֽל־הַנָּחָשׁ֮ כִּ֣י עָשִׂ֣יתָ זֹּאת֒ אָר֤וּר אַתָּה֙ מִכׇּל־הַבְּהֵמָ֔ה וּמִכֹּ֖ל חַיַּ֣ת הַשָּׂדֶ֑ה עַל־גְּחֹנְךָ֣ תֵלֵ֔ךְ וְעָפָ֥ר תֹּאכַ֖ל כׇּל־יְמֵ֥י חַיֶּֽיךָ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Then the ETERNAL God said to the serpent,“Because you did this,More cursed shall you beThan all cattleAnd all the wild beasts:On your belly shall you crawlAnd dirt shall you eatAll the days of your life.
sections.text: Then the ETERNAL God said to the serpent,“Because you did this,More cursed shall you beThan all cattleAnd all the wild beasts:On your belly shall you crawlAnd dirt shall you eatAll the days of your life.
sections.text_simple: Then the ETERNAL God said to the serpent,“Because you did this,More cursed shall you beThan all cattleAnd all the wild beasts:On your belly shall you crawlAnd dirt shall you eatAll the days of your life.

sections.aramaic: וְאֵיבָ֣ה ׀ אָשִׁ֗ית בֵּֽינְךָ֙ וּבֵ֣ין הָֽאִשָּׁ֔ה וּבֵ֥ין זַרְעֲךָ֖ וּבֵ֣ין זַרְעָ֑הּ ה֚וּא יְשׁוּפְךָ֣ רֹ֔אשׁ וְאַתָּ֖ה תְּשׁוּפֶ֥נּוּ עָקֵֽב׃ {ס}
sections.aramaic_translation: I will put enmityBetween you and the woman,And between your offspring and hers;They shall strike at your head,And you shall strike at their heel.”
sections.text: I will put enmityBetween you and the woman,And between your offspring and hers;They shall strike at your head,And you shall strike at their heel.”
sections.text_simple: I will put enmityBetween you and the woman,And between your offspring and hers;They shall strike at your head,And you shall strike at their heel.”

sections.aramaic: אֶֽל־הָאִשָּׁ֣ה אָמַ֗ר הַרְבָּ֤ה אַרְבֶּה֙ עִצְּבוֹנֵ֣ךְ וְהֵֽרֹנֵ֔ךְ בְּעֶ֖צֶב תֵּֽלְדִ֣י בָנִ֑ים וְאֶל־אִישֵׁךְ֙ תְּשׁ֣וּקָתֵ֔ךְ וְה֖וּא יִמְשׇׁל־בָּֽךְ׃ {ס}
sections.aramaic_translation: And to the woman [God] said,“I will greatly expandYour toil—and your pregnancies;In hardship shall you bear children.Yet your urge shall be for your husband,And his will shall prevail over yours.”
sections.text: And to the woman [God] said,“I will greatly expandYour toil—and your pregnancies;In hardship shall you bear children.Yet your urge shall be for your husband,And his will shall prevail over yours.”
sections.text_simple: And to the woman [God] said,“I will greatly expandYour toil—and your pregnancies;In hardship shall you bear children.Yet your urge shall be for your husband,And his will shall prevail over yours.”

sections.aramaic: וּלְאָדָ֣ם אָמַ֗ר כִּֽי־שָׁמַ֘עְתָּ֮ לְק֣וֹל אִשְׁתֶּ֒ךָ֒ וַתֹּ֙אכַל֙ מִן־הָעֵ֔ץ אֲשֶׁ֤ר צִוִּיתִ֙יךָ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר לֹ֥א תֹאכַ֖ל מִמֶּ֑נּוּ אֲרוּרָ֤ה הָֽאֲדָמָה֙ בַּֽעֲבוּרֶ֔ךָ בְּעִצָּבוֹן֙ תֹּֽאכְלֶ֔נָּה כֹּ֖ל יְמֵ֥י חַיֶּֽיךָ׃
sections.aramaic_translation: To Adam [God] said, “Because you did as your wife said and ate of the tree about which I commanded you, ‘You shall not eat of it,’Cursed be the ground because of you;By toil shall you eat of itAll the days of your life:
sections.text: To Adam [God] said, “Because you did as your wife said and ate of the tree about which I commanded you, ‘You shall not eat of it,’Cursed be the ground because of you;By toil shall you eat of itAll the days of your life:
sections.text_simple: To Adam [God] said, “Because you did as your wife said and ate of the tree about which I commanded you, ‘You shall not eat of it,’Cursed be the ground because of you;By toil shall you eat of itAll the days of your life:

sections.aramaic: וְק֥וֹץ וְדַרְדַּ֖ר תַּצְמִ֣יחַֽ לָ֑ךְ וְאָכַלְתָּ֖ אֶת־עֵ֥שֶׂב הַשָּׂדֶֽה׃
sections.aramaic_translation: Thorns and thistles shall it sprout for you.But your food shall be the grasses of the field;
sections.text: Thorns and thistles shall it sprout for you.But your food shall be the grasses of the field;
sections.text_simple: Thorns and thistles shall it sprout for you.But your food shall be the grasses of the field;

sections.aramaic: בְּזֵעַ֤ת אַפֶּ֙יךָ֙ תֹּ֣אכַל לֶ֔חֶם עַ֤ד שֽׁוּבְךָ֙ אֶל־הָ֣אֲדָמָ֔ה כִּ֥י מִמֶּ֖נָּה לֻקָּ֑חְתָּ כִּֽי־עָפָ֣ר אַ֔תָּה וְאֶל־עָפָ֖ר תָּשֽׁוּב׃
sections.aramaic_translation: By the sweat of your browShall you get bread to eat,Until you return to the ground—For from it you were taken.For dustbdust Heb. ʻaphar, as in 2.7; cf. the second note there. you are,And to dust you shall return.”
sections.text: By the sweat of your browShall you get bread to eat,Until you return to the ground—For from it you were taken.For dustbdust Heb. ʻaphar, as in 2.7; cf. the second note there. you are,And to dust you shall return.”
sections.text_simple: By the sweat of your browShall you get bread to eat,Until you return to the ground—For from it you were taken.For dustbdust Heb. ʻaphar, as in 2.7; cf. the second note there. you are,And to dust you shall return.”

sections.aramaic: וַיִּקְרָ֧א הָֽאָדָ֛ם שֵׁ֥ם אִשְׁתּ֖וֹ חַוָּ֑ה כִּ֛י הִ֥וא הָֽיְתָ֖ה אֵ֥ם כׇּל־חָֽי׃
sections.aramaic_translation: The Human named his wife Eve,cEve Heb. ḥawwah. because she was the mother of all the living.dliving Heb. ḥai.
sections.text: The Human named his wife Eve,cEve Heb. ḥawwah. because she was the mother of all the living.dliving Heb. ḥai.
sections.text_simple: The Human named his wife Eve,cEve Heb. ḥawwah. because she was the mother of all the living.dliving Heb. ḥai.

sections.aramaic: וַיַּ֩עַשׂ֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֜ים לְאָדָ֧ם וּלְאִשְׁתּ֛וֹ כׇּתְנ֥וֹת ע֖וֹר וַיַּלְבִּשֵֽׁם׃ {פ}
sections.aramaic_translation: And the ETERNAL God made garments of skins for Adam and his wife, and clothed them.
sections.text: And the ETERNAL God made garments of skins for Adam and his wife, and clothed them.
sections.text_simple: And the ETERNAL God made garments of skins for Adam and his wife, and clothed them.

sections.aramaic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ׀ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֗ים הֵ֤ן הָֽאָדָם֙ הָיָה֙ כְּאַחַ֣ד מִמֶּ֔נּוּ לָדַ֖עַת ט֣וֹב וָרָ֑ע וְעַתָּ֣ה ׀ פֶּן־יִשְׁלַ֣ח יָד֗וֹ וְלָקַח֙ גַּ֚ם מֵעֵ֣ץ הַֽחַיִּ֔ים וְאָכַ֖ל וָחַ֥י לְעֹלָֽם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: And the ETERNAL God said, “Now that humankind has become like any of us, knowing good and bad, what if one should stretch out a hand and take also from the tree of life and eat, and live forever!”
sections.text: And the ETERNAL God said, “Now that humankind has become like any of us, knowing good and bad, what if one should stretch out a hand and take also from the tree of life and eat, and live forever!”
sections.text_simple: And the ETERNAL God said, “Now that humankind has become like any of us, knowing good and bad, what if one should stretch out a hand and take also from the tree of life and eat, and live forever!”

sections.aramaic: וַֽיְשַׁלְּחֵ֛הוּ יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים מִגַּן־עֵ֑דֶן לַֽעֲבֹד֙ אֶת־הָ֣אֲדָמָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר לֻקַּ֖ח מִשָּֽׁם׃
sections.aramaic_translation: So the ETERNAL God banished them from the garden of Eden, to till the humusehumus Heb. ʼadamah, lit. “soil,” as in 2.7; cf. the second note there. from which they were taken:
sections.text: So the ETERNAL God banished them from the garden of Eden, to till the humusehumus Heb. ʼadamah, lit. “soil,” as in 2.7; cf. the second note there. from which they were taken:
sections.text_simple: So the ETERNAL God banished them from the garden of Eden, to till the humusehumus Heb. ʼadamah, lit. “soil,” as in 2.7; cf. the second note there. from which they were taken:

sections.aramaic: וַיְגָ֖רֶשׁ אֶת־הָֽאָדָ֑ם וַיַּשְׁכֵּן֩ מִקֶּ֨דֶם לְגַן־עֵ֜דֶן אֶת־הַכְּרֻבִ֗ים וְאֵ֨ת לַ֤הַט הַחֶ֙רֶב֙ הַמִּתְהַפֶּ֔כֶת לִשְׁמֹ֕ר אֶת־דֶּ֖רֶךְ עֵ֥ץ הַֽחַיִּֽים׃ {ס}
sections.aramaic_translation: humankind was driven out; and east of the garden of Eden were stationed the cherubim and the fiery ever-turning sword, to guard the way to the tree of life.
sections.text: humankind was driven out; and east of the garden of Eden were stationed the cherubim and the fiery ever-turning sword, to guard the way to the tree of life.
sections.text_simple: humankind was driven out; and east of the garden of Eden were stationed the cherubim and the fiery ever-turning sword, to guard the way to the tree of life.
